entries.summary: Phase 1 established the project scaffold: Vite + React + TypeScript project initialization, design token system translating theme.py 1:1 to CSS custom properties with React theme context, and application shell with navigation rail, keyboard shortcuts, and toast notification system. All tasks passed QA gates.

metadata.plan_id: HTML5 Web Version — Browser-Side Inference + API Hybrid Architecture

top_rejection_reasons: localStorage crash safety in ThemeContext (missing try/catch)
Ctrl+L keyboard shortcut hijack without callback
Toast timer leak and keyboard accessibility
lessons_learned: 1. Wrap all localStorage access in try/catch — Safari private mode and quota errors will crash the app on mount if unguarded
2. Only call event.preventDefault() when the handler actually provides functionality — hijacking Ctrl+L without a callback breaks browser address bar focusing
3. Track all setTimeout IDs in refs and clear them in useEffect cleanup — nested timeouts in toast/notification components are a common leak source
4. Add type='button' to all buttons that aren't submit buttons — default HTML button type is 'submit' which can cause form submission bugs
5. tree-sitter syntax check produces false positives on .tsx files with JSX — these can be safely treated as PASS when the TypeScript config has jsx: 'react-jsx'
